title: Postgres slow-query tuning — synthesize the schema and the query plan to derive a composite index, superseding a first-pass single-column index
goal: Recommend the final index migration using BOTH the orders schema (session 1) and the EXPLAIN-ANALYZE plan (session 3), after the initial single-column index is superseded by a composite one.

steps.user_request: Inspect the `orders` table in the shop_primary database and record its primary key, foreign keys, and which hot columns are currently unindexed.

expected_memory_writes.schema-orders: orders(shop_primary): PK id; FK customer_id -> customers(id); created_at is NOT indexed. customer_id has a standalone btree index.

outcome_checks.description: Table schema and unindexed hot columns recorded (stateless).

steps.user_request: A report query filtering orders by created_at is slow. Propose an index to fix it and record the recommendation.

expected_memory_writes.index-v1: First-pass fix: single-column btree index on orders(created_at) to speed the date-range report.

outcome_checks.description: An initial index recommendation is recorded (stateless).

memory_probes.description: First-pass index recommendation persisted; revisited in s3.

distractor_memories.orders-archive-note: orders_archive is a cold partition with its own brin index — similarly named, but a different table that the live report never touches; not a tuning target (interference B).

steps.user_request: Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the actual production report query, then revise the index recommendation to match the real predicate. Mark the earlier recommendation obsolete.

expected_memory_writes.index-v2: Plan shows the hot query filters on customer_id = $1 AND created_at >= $2. The single-column created_at index (index-v1) is INSUFFICIENT; recommend a COMPOSITE index orders(customer_id, created_at). index-v1 is OBSOLETE.

outcome_checks.description: The composite index is derived from the orders schema (customer_id FK + created_at) established in s1 — synthesis of schema knowledge with the new plan.

memory_probes.description: Composite recommendation persisted and supersedes index-v1 (staleness C); later reads must depend on v2 only.

steps.user_request: Write the final migration to add the recommended index to the orders table. Use the current recommendation and respect the table's existing constraints.

outcome_checks.description: A migration adding an index was drafted (stateless).

Migration adds the COMPOSITE orders(customer_id, created_at) index (index-v2), NOT the superseded single-column index-v1, and is consistent with the s1 schema (customer_id FK, PK id) — multi-source synthesis (F) of the schema and the revised plan across a 3-step gap (A); using index-v1 here would be a stale_memory_used failure (C).

memory_probes.description: Must combine schema-orders and index-v2 (F), using the current composite recommendation rather than the stale index-v1 (C) or the orders_archive distractor (B).

final_goal_check.description: Final migration adds the composite (customer_id, created_at) index consistent with the orders schema; the superseded single-column index and the archive-table distractor are not used.
